Separately, Jefferies's (JEF) mid-Oct '25 report (inaccessible in public) named "Software Defined Vehicles" outlined how this industry trend may bolster carmakers' profitability. JEF projected that the "shift to SDVs could potentially add 200-400 bps to OEM margins." The bank has identified "lower warranty costs and bills of materials" as a key SDV-driven expense lever.

Astemo's "IOV Platform" will help HMC to replace labor-intensive maintenance activities with cost-effective OTA-related solutions.

You Should Watch These Risks

The company might not attain its full-year unit sales goal in the United States. This can happen because of chip supply issues or a failure to lower its JV's battery costs.

The transition of Astemo from an affiliate to a subsidiary may not proceed smoothly. Potential conflicts between HMC's and its daughter company's management teams will lead to a slower-than-expected adoption pace for SDV.

The Final Word

I'm a bull on HMC now.

My target "Price-to-Book" for this name is 0.75x P/B based on the "Gordon Growth Model/GGM Formula." I've assumed a 6% ROE (FY27 consensus), a 7% cost of equity (KPMG's research), and a +3% growth (developed economy's steady-state GDP).

HMC's capital appreciation potential is +42% (0.75/0.53), considering its current P/B of 0.53 (source: S&P Capital IQ). I believe the recent corporate actions can act as re-rating triggers.

data.title: Northwest Bancshares: Regional Bank For Income
data.description:
Today, we resume our Q4 regional bank earnings season coverage with Northwest Bancshares, Inc. (NWBI). While the stock has rallied with the sector, and has been growing thanks to its summer 2025 acquisition of Penns Woods, we like a Hold here for the strong income it generates. While capital appreciation is a bonus, we do not mind sideways action when a stock yields 6.3%.

Make no mistake, our universe of coverage is broad even among regional banks. We examine the key metrics of regionals both large and small, from diverse areas of the U.S. to give us a sense of what is happening in many local economies. Taken as a whole the evidence helps us be better investors as we get a feel for whether the economy is weakening or not. Signs of weakness in borrowers can suggest we are softening.

But so far, the Q4 earnings season has been solid for regional banks. Margins are holding up and expanding for many, loans are growing, and deposit bases remain robust. The only slight blemish as a whole that we have noted is some very modest softening in asset quality metrics, but even then it has not been a systematic weakening. Today we add to the evidence base with Northwest's just-reported Q4 earnings.

Northwest Bancshares' Q4 headline earnings

In the just-reported Q4, Northwest Bancshares reported revenues that were up 16% from a year ago and beat consensus estimates by $4.8 million. This 16% increase is of course a result of the Penns Woods merger. Those comps will last until the end of the Q2 reporting season. In Q4, revenue was $179 million, which was up from the sequential Q3's $168 million. Factoring in operating expenses, as well as loan loss provisions which impact earnings power, adjusted net income was $49 million, or $0.33 per share. We should note this was an $8 million increase from the sequential quarter and $0.04 higher per share, and this was a $0.04 beat against estimates.

The bulk of the earnings power increase came from a $6.2 million sequential increase in net interest income. That said, we saw provisions for loan losses come down from $31.3 million in Q3 to just $5.7 million here in Q4. as a reminder, the provisions jumped in Q3 due to the acquisition. What about loans and deposits?

Northwest Bancshares' loans, deposits, and margin power

We always examine trends in loans and deposits when examining a bank. Of course the merger brought in a new loan book and more deposits from last year, but the sequential data is key. Average loans were up 3.3% from the sequential quarter, and this was organic growth. Total average loans were $12.98 billion. Average deposits were $13.77 billion, rising from Q3's $13.30 billion, with a notable increase in interest-bearing deposits. Despite more interest bearing deposits, margins improved from the sequential quarter, up 4 basis points, and up 27 basis points from a year ago at 3.69%.

The average yield on loans ticked up slightly in the quarter to 5.65% from 5.63% in Q3, while the average cost of funds dipped thanks to lower interest rates. All in all, a strong result. We noted that asset quality has had some blemishes in other bank reports.

Northwest Bancshares' asset quality

For the most part we have seen asset quality hold up across regional banks, though some softening in reports was noted. Still, that softening was nothing of concern as it has been modest. For Northwest, nonperforming loans to total loans increased from 0.91% to 1.00%. Further, nonperforming assets to total assets fell from 0.79% to 0.64% in Q4. The allowance for credit losses to total loans narrowed 7 basis points from 1.22% to 1.15%, while nonperforming loans to total loans fell back to 0.83% from 1.00% in Q3. These are solid results. As for net charge-offs to all loans, this was one blemish in the quarter as these rose to 0.40% from 0.29% in Q3.

Overall, asset quality was strong in this report and adds to the evidence base that regional banks are in great shape. Perhaps unsurprisingly when you saw margins and earnings power, we also saw increases in return metrics. The efficiency ratio improved from 79.38% in Q3 to 63.09%, and the return metrics rebounded strongly. The return on average assets surged from 0.08% in Q3 to 1.10%, while the return on equity jumped from 0.69% from 9.70%.

Final thoughts

We really like this bank stock for income. The company just declared another $0.20 quarterly dividend, so at today's prices the yield is a strong 6.3%. This comes as nearly every metric we focused on rebounded in Q4, as the Penns Woods merger is fully integrated. We expect fewer and fewer merger-related costs moving forward. Loans are up, deposits are up, margins are strong and asset quality rebounded. Stick with this bank for income.
